steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence to complete reads: It is incorrect to assume, ______ that Elara is the seventh moon from Jupiter in terms of proximity. The prior sentences explain that Roman numerals on Jupiter's moons indicate naming order, not distance. The blank must introduce a conclusion drawn from those facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — however,: Signals contrast with the previous idea. The sentence is not contrasting — it is inferring from the prior fact. ✗
  • Choice B — then,: Signals a conclusion or consequence. Because the numerals indicate naming order rather than proximity, it then follows that assuming Elara is the seventh-closest moon is incorrect. ✓
  • Choice C — moreover,: Adds a new related point rather than drawing a conclusion. The sentence wraps up the reasoning, not adding to it. ✗
  • Choice D — finally,: Closes an enumerated sequence. No sequence of steps is being completed here. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
